codeReview.summary: Implementation successfully delivers the Database abstraction layer with connection management, context manager support, and comprehensive CRUD methods. All 35 unit tests pass (originally 25, expanded to 35 in later commits). Code quality is high with proper error handling, logging, and documentation. However, the commit includes several out-of-scope changes to unrelated files that should have been in separate commits.

issues.description: Enhanced error logging for token refresh was added (lines 213-225). While this improves observability, it's out of scope for m1-f1-database-class which should only implement the Database class itself. This change belongs in a separate commit or a different feature focused on error handling improvements.

Added proactive token validation before requests (lines 203-211) and improved 403 error handling with detailed error info (lines 244-261). These enhancements are out of scope for the database abstraction feature and should be in a separate commit focused on HTTP client improvements.

Added global exception handler for ExceptionGroup simplification (lines 444-477). This is unrelated to database abstraction and should be in a separate commit. While useful, it's scope creep for this feature.
Added a 394-line log file that appears to be debugging output. This should not be committed to the repository at all - it should be in .gitignore or deleted.

Added initialization script for the mission. While useful for the mission infrastructure, this is not part of the m1-f1-database-class feature implementation and should have been committed separately as mission setup.
Added skill documentation (192 lines). This is mission infrastructure, not part of the database class feature. Should be in a separate setup commit.
Added skill documentation (147 lines). This is mission infrastructure, not part of the database class feature. Should be in a separate setup commit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker committed multiple unrelated changes in a single commit. The commit includes the Database class implementation (in scope) plus error handling improvements in auth.py, http_client.py, main.py (out of scope), plus mission infrastructure files (.factory/skills/, .factory/init.sh, .factory/services.yaml), plus a debug log file (Untitled-1.ini). AGENTS.md doesn't explicitly state that commits should be focused on a single feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit 37781df0c9c7cfa3244140e2d781ea5d06e09b0b modified 14 files: .factory/init.sh, .factory/library/user-testing.md, .factory/services.yaml, .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md, .factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md, Untitled-1.ini (394 lines of logs), kiro/core/auth.py, kiro/core/database.py, kiro/core/http_client.py, kiro/kiro_errors.py, kiro/providers/kiro_provider.py, kiro/streaming/core.py, main.py, tests/unit/test_database.py. Only kiro/core/database.py and tests/unit/test_database.py are directly related to m1-f1-database-class.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker committed a debug/log file (Untitled-1.ini) containing 394 lines of error logs and stack traces. This file should not be in version control. AGENTS.md mentions 'Remove any debug print statements' in cleanup but doesn't explicitly mention not committing log files.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: File Untitled-1.ini added in commit 37781df0c9c7cfa3244140e2d781ea5d06e09b0b contains logs like '2026-03-16 15:14:07 | ERROR | kiro.utils_pkg.parsers:_finalize_tool_call:430 - Tool call truncated' and exception stack traces. This appears to be a debugging artifact.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill procedure was followed correctly for the core feature (TDD approach, comprehensive tests, manual verification). However, the skill doesn't provide guidance on commit hygiene - specifically about keeping commits focused on a single feature and not committing debug artifacts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows proper TDD workflow: tests written first, implementation followed, 25 tests passing (later expanded to 35). However, the commit includes many out-of-scope changes. The skill's 'Clean Up' section (step 5) mentions removing debug print statements but doesn't mention commit scope or avoiding debug file commits.

summary: Feature m1-f1-database-class successfully implements the Database abstraction layer with all required functionality. The implementation is high quality with comprehensive test coverage (35 tests, all passing), proper error handling, good documentation, and correct fulfillment of assertions m1-a1 (importable class), m1-a2 (connection management), m1-a3 (context manager support), and m1-a7 (error handling). The Database class provides all required CRUD methods (execute, fetch_one, fetch_all, insert, update, delete) and works both as a context manager and in standalone mode. However, the commit contains significant scope creep with 7 out of 14 modified files being unrelated to the database class feature, including error handling improvements in other modules, mission infrastructure setup, and a debug log file that shouldn't be committed. These issues are non-blocking as they don't affect the correctness of the Database class implementation itself, but they indicate a need for clearer conventions around commit hygiene and scope management.
